title: ～にともなって
titleEn: Expresses along with or as a result of; indicates that one event occurs along with another
structure: Noun (event) + にともなって, Verb-casual + にともなって
structureEn: Noun (event) + にともなって, Verb-casual + にともなって
explanation: Dùng khi một sự việc xảy ra cùng với sự thay đổi hoặc diễn biến khác, hoặc là hệ quả đi kèm của nó. Có thể hiểu là “cùng với”, “theo đó”, “kéo theo”. Cấu trúc: Noun (event) + にともなって, Verb-casual + にともなって.
explanationEn: The ～にともなって grammar point is used to indicate that one event occurs along with another event, or as a result of another event. It can be translated as along with, as a result of, or in accordance with in English
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～にほかならない
titleEn: Express that something is nothing but or none other than
structure: Verb-plain + にほかならない, い-Adjective + にほかならない, な-Adjective + にほかならない, Noun + にほかならない
structureEn: Verb-plain + にほかならない, い-Adjective + にほかならない, な-Adjective + にほかならない, Noun + にほかならない
explanation: Dùng để nhấn mạnh rằng sự việc chính là điều được nêu, không gì khác. Sắc thái khẳng định mạnh: “chính là”, “không gì khác ngoài”. Cấu trúc: Verb-plain + にほかならない, い-Adjective + にほかならない, な-Adjective + にほかならない, Noun + にほかならない.
explanationEn: The ～にほかならない grammar point is used to emphasize that something is no more or no less than what is specified. It can be seen as a strong way to say that something is nothing but or none other than
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～にもかかわらず
titleEn: Expresses despite, in spite of or even though
structure: Verb (any tense/form) + にもかかわらず, い-Adjective + にもかかわらず, な-Adjective (or Noun) + であるにもかかわらず
structureEn: Verb (any tense/form) + にもかかわらず, い-Adjective + にもかかわらず, な-Adjective (or Noun) + であるにもかかわらず
explanation: Dùng khi kết quả hoặc tình huống phía sau xảy ra trái với điều đáng lẽ dự đoán từ vế trước. Sắc thái trang trọng: “mặc dù”, “bất chấp”. Cấu trúc: Verb (any tense/form) + にもかかわらず, い-Adjective + にもかかわらず, な-Adjective (or Noun) + であるにもかかわらず.
explanationEn: The ～にもかかわらず grammar point is used to express that something occurs, or a situation exists, despite or in spite of a contrasting or unfavorable condition. It can be translated as despite, in spite of, or even though in English
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～により
titleEn: Indicates the reason, method, or means by which something happens; by, through, due to
structure: Noun + により, Verb + により
structureEn: Noun + により, Verb + により
explanation: Dùng trong văn trang trọng để nêu nguyên nhân, phương tiện, cách thức hoặc tác nhân khiến một việc xảy ra. Có thể hiểu là “do”, “bởi”, “thông qua”, “bằng”. Cấu trúc: Noun + により, Verb + により.
explanationEn: The ～により grammar point is used to indicate the reason, method, or means by which something happens or is done. It can be often translated as by, through, or due to in English. It can be attached directly to nouns or verbs in plain form
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～にわたって
titleEn: Indicates a range or extent; over, throughout, across
explanation: Dùng để chỉ phạm vi trải dài trên một khoảng thời gian, không gian, số lượng hoặc lĩnh vực. Có thể hiểu là “trong suốt”, “trên khắp”, “kéo dài qua”. Cấu trúc: Noun + にわたって.
explanationEn: The ～にわたって grammar point is used to express a range or extent of a particular action, event, or situation. It can be translated as over, throughout, or across in English. This structure can be used with various types of words such as time, distance, and quantity
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～に先立ち
titleEn: Express an action or event that occurs before another; before, prior to, preceding
structure: Noun + に先立ち / Verb-dictionary form + に先立って
structureEn: Noun + に先立ち / Verb-dictionary form + に先立って
explanation: Dùng trong ngữ cảnh trang trọng để nói một việc được thực hiện trước sự kiện chính. Có thể hiểu là “trước khi”, “trước thềm”. Cấu trúc: Noun + に先立ち / Verb-dictionary form + に先立って.
explanationEn: The ～に先立ち grammar point is used to indicate that a certain action or event occurs before another action or event. It can be translated as before, prior to, or preceding in English. It is often used with nouns (Noun + に先立ち) or with dictionary-form verbs (Verb-dictionary form + に先立って)
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～に反して
titleEn: Contrary to, against, in spite of
explanation: Dùng khi kết quả hoặc hành động trái với dự đoán, mong đợi, quy tắc hoặc ý muốn đã nêu. Có thể hiểu là “trái với”, “ngược lại với”. Cấu trúc: Noun + に反して.
explanationEn: The ～に反して grammar point is used to express that something is contrary to or against an expectation or belief. It can be translated as contrary to, against, or in spite of in English. It is generally used with nouns
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～に基づいて
titleEn: Based on, according to, depending on
explanation: Dùng khi một hành động, quyết định hoặc nhận định dựa trên một cơ sở như dữ liệu, quy định, kinh nghiệm, nguyên tắc. Có thể hiểu là “dựa trên”, “căn cứ vào”. Cấu trúc: Noun + に基づいて.
explanationEn: The ～に基づいて grammar point is used to express that something is based on, according to, or depending on a certain source or foundation. It can be used with nouns that represent a source, rule, principle, or standard
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)
